manual.title: Manual
manual.description: A guide to the platform — what each section does and how to use it.

gettingStarted.title: Getting started
gettingStarted.p1: MicroCred lets your institution design, issue and manage verifiable micro-credentials. Every issued credential is anchored on the blockchain so anyone can independently verify it.
gettingStarted.p2: Below you'll find more detail on each part of the platform. Use the "Open …" links to jump straight to the matching page.
gettingStarted.staffNote: As a staff member you see the sections relevant to the micro-credentials you are assigned to.

overview.title: Overview
overview.body: Institution dashboard with key metrics (published micro-credentials, issued credentials, active learners and staff), credential lifecycle, pending actions, blockchain status and recent activity.

templates.title: Micro-credentials
templates.body: Catalog of micro-credential templates for your institution. Admins see all templates; staff see only the templates they are assigned to. Open a template to view requirements, evidence, supervisors and quality-assurance documents.

templateNew.title: Create Micro-credential
templateNew.body: Define a new template: title, description, level, learning outcomes, prerequisites, evidence requirements, supervisors, stackability and quality-assurance documents. Publish when ready so learners can apply.

staff.title: Staff
staff.body: Manage staff members in your institution and assign them to specific micro-credentials. Assigned staff can review applications and issue credentials only for their templates.

earners.title: Earners
earners.body: Directory of learners who have applied for or received credentials from your institution. Review their history and issued credentials.

requests.title: Issuance Requests
requests.body: Incoming applications. Review submitted evidence, request changes, mark as "Evidence collected" and "Verified", then issue the credential. You can also reject with a reason — the earner can edit and resend.

issue.title: Direct Issuance
issue.body: Issue a credential directly to a single earner without an application — useful for invited recipients or pre-verified achievements.

bulk.title: Bulk Issuance
bulk.body: Upload a CSV to issue the same micro-credential to many earners at once. Validate the file, review the preview, then submit the batch.

credentials.title: Issued Credentials
credentials.body: All credentials your institution has issued. Use search (by earner, title or ID) and filters (template, lifecycle status) to narrow the list — the search box has an "x" to reset it. The first time you open this page a short guided tour walks you through the available actions. Per-row actions depend on the lifecycle: "Edit & resend" for rejected credentials (update grade and/or expiry, then resend to the earner for acceptance); "Accept rejection" to permanently delete a rejected credential; "Renew expiry" for issued or expired credentials with an expiry date (walks through review → evidence collected → verified, then issues the new expiry on chain without requiring earner acceptance).

revocations.title: Revocations
revocations.body: Revoke previously issued credentials with a reason. Revocations are anchored on the blockchain so verifiers always see the current status.

anchoring.title: Blockchain Queue
anchoring.body: Anchoring status of issued credentials: pending, submitted, confirmed or failed. Retry failed anchors and inspect transaction details.

profile.title: Public Profile
profile.body: Public page of your institution that learners and employers can visit. Lists your published micro-credentials and lets visitors verify any credential you have issued.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.body: Updates on new applications, evidence submissions, anchoring failures, revocations and other relevant events.

settings.title: Settings
settings.body: Account settings, security, blockchain configuration and notification preferences.
